mappings.rationale: Directly covers the Cold War including the Iron Curtain, NATO formation, Berlin blockade, containment policy, and detente discussed extensively in subsections C.2 and C.3.
Directly covers World War II including German conquests, the Pacific war, Allied strategy, and the defeat of the Axis Powers discussed in subsection B.
Provides essential background for the post-WWI problems, the League of Nations, and the interwar diplomatic failures discussed in subsection A.
Directly relevant to the entire section's focus on international movements, diplomacy, Cold War dynamics, and post-Cold War world order.
Relevant to the security architecture discussed including NATO, the balance of terror, nuclear deterrence, and post-Cold War security challenges.
Directly relevant to the establishment and role of the UN discussed in subsections C.2.a and C.3, including UN peacekeeping and decolonization efforts.
Relevant to European integration movements discussed in subsection C.3.b.ii, including the economic recovery and political integration of western Europe.
Directly relevant to the atomic and hydrogen bombs, the balance of terror, France as a nuclear power, and arms buildup discussed in subsections B.6, C.2.a, and C.2.f.viii.
Relevant to the rise of fascist movements in the interwar period, the Axis Powers, and the ideological dimension of World War II discussed in subsections A and B.
Relevant to the spread of communism, Soviet expansion, the Cold War ideological conflict, and the disintegration of the Soviet Union discussed throughout subsection C.
Directly relevant to Soviet diplomatic isolation, the Nazi-Soviet pact, Soviet expansion in eastern Europe, and the collapse of the USSR discussed throughout subsections A-C.
Directly relevant to international terrorism discussed in subsection C.3.c.iv as a significant area of conflict in the contemporary world.
Directly covers the Popular Front and the Spanish Civil War (1936-39) discussed in subsection A.3.b as a precursor to World War II.
Directly relevant to the diplomatic history covered throughout, from the Locarno agreements to the Munich agreement to Cold War diplomacy and detente.
Relevant to the geopolitical dynamics of the interwar period, Cold War spheres of influence, and the post-Cold War reconfiguration discussed throughout subsection C.
Relevant to the evolution of warfare from WWI through WWII and the Cold War era conflicts including Korea, Vietnam, and the Middle East discussed throughout.
Relevant to strategic plans and goals of both the Axis Powers and Allies discussed in subsections B.4 and B.5.
Relevant to the development of military aviation technology including the Battle of Britain and atomic bombing discussed in subsections B.1 and B.6.
Directly relevant to the burgeoning of military technology including communications, naval ships, aircraft, missiles, and atomic bombs discussed in subsection B.6.

mappings.vsiTitle: The Great Depression and The New Deal
mappings.rationale: Relevant to the economic impact of the Great Depression in the 1930s and the New Deal policy in the U.S. discussed in subsection A.4.
Relevant to the post-Cold War international economic and political order, decolonization, and the emergence of new states discussed in subsection C.3.
Directly relevant to the Arab-Israeli wars, Palestinian question, and the role of the Middle East in international conflict discussed in subsections C.2.e and C.3.c.ii.
Directly relevant to the postwar decolonization, increased demands for self-determination, and establishment of new states discussed in subsections A.3.c and C.3.a.ii.
Relevant to the rise of nationalist movements among subject peoples demanding self-determination discussed in subsection A.3.c and throughout the postwar period.
Directly relevant to the aftermath of the Paris Peace Conference, the enforcement of the peace settlement, and Germany's reaction discussed in subsections A.1-2.
Directly relevant to the establishment of Nazi Germany, rearmament, expansion in eastern Europe, and the ideological dimensions of WWII discussed in subsections A.4.c and B.
Relevant to the wartime atrocities and the concentration camp system that was a major feature of the World War II period discussed in subsection B.
Relevant to the various peace attempts discussed including the League of Nations, the Kellogg-Briand agreement, the UN, and Helsinki conference.
Relevant to the development of international legal frameworks from the League of Nations to the United Nations discussed throughout the section.
Relevant to the human rights dimensions of decolonization, the UN framework, and post-Cold War international order discussed in subsection C.3.
Relevant to the rise of populist and authoritarian movements in the interwar period and their contribution to the collapse of democratic governments discussed in subsection A.1.a.
Relevant to the ideological dimensions of both World Wars and the Cold War, including fascism, communism, and liberal democracy discussed throughout the section.
Relevant to the revolutionary movements and political upheavals discussed throughout including Soviet communism, national liberation movements, and the Cuban Revolution.
Relevant to the numerous civil wars discussed including the Spanish Civil War, Chinese civil war, and civil conflicts in Sub-Saharan Africa noted in subsection C.3.c.iii.
Relevant to the massive displacement of peoples during WWII, the Cold War, and post-colonial conflicts discussed throughout the section.
Relevant to the civil war in China and communist rule discussed in subsection C.2.d.ii.
Relevant to the post-Cold War rise of emerging powers and the Third World as a zone of conflict discussed in subsection C.3.a.
Relevant to the post-Cold War economic order, movements toward free markets, and economic transitions in eastern Europe discussed in subsection C.3.b.iv.
Relevant to the dictatorships in central and eastern Europe in the interwar period and the authoritarian communist regimes during the Cold War discussed throughout.
Covers international humanitarian law, intervention, refugees, and peacebuilding — relevant to this section's treatment of international movements and diplomacy since 1920.
